arrival.say: Good evening, sir. Welcome to the Hôtel Bombe.
Grey. Earl Grey. I believe I'm expected — by someone.

look.text: A silver bowl of cocktail olives. One of them has clear leadership qualities.

take.text: You palm the ranking olive, pick and all. For later. Espionage is mostly confiscating snacks.

look.text: Abandoned aperitifs. Among the wreckage: one immaculate glass swizzle stick.

take.text: You liberate the swizzle stick and dry it on a napkin, like a professional.

look.text: A chandelier the size of a modest yacht. You resist the professional urge to swing from it. For now.
The table stands abandoned. The house, for once, is not winning.
Green baize, ivory chips, and odds curated personally by the croupier.

use.text: You are on duty. Also, you have seen the croupier count. You would lose the tuxedo.

look.text: White jacket, perfect posture, eyes that have seen everything and billed for none of it.

itemUse.speaker: target
itemUse.text: Sir wishes to RETURN an olive? Sir is bold. Keep it, sir. I insist.

look.text: The croupier. Waistcoat, welding goggles pushed into her hair — an unusual combination for card games.

exits.name: terrace doors
exits.look: Tall glass doors onto the cliff terrace. Sea air and, faintly, funicular grease.
